calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jordan Sussman of Zief, Diment & Glickman telephone number 415-986-3644 for Plaintiff.
Geoffrey MacBride of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney telephone number 415-788-1900 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tenative ruling.
DEFENDANTS FIRST BANKS, INC. and 1000 TARAVAL, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Under the trivial defect doctrine, a property owner is not liable for damages caused by minor, trivial, or insignificant defects in property. (Stathoulis v. City of Montebello (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 559, 566.) In determining whether a defect is trivial, the court reviews the evidence regarding the type and size of the defect and if the preliminary analysis shows that the defect is trivial the court considers whether any additional factors bear on the defect's dangerousness. (Id. at 567.) As Defendant notes, the case law on sidewalk trivial defects stands for the position that any sidewalk elevation difference less than one inch is a trivial defect as a matter of law. (See Fielder, supra, 71 Cal.App.3d at p. 726; Caloroso v. Hathaway (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 922; Cadam v. Somerset Gardens Townhouses HOA (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 383, 389.) Here, contrary to Defendants' position the court cannot conclude as a matter of law that the defect is trivial because Plaintiff has presented evidence which raises a disputed issue of material fact as to whether the sidewalk elevation difference is less than one inch. (Plaintiff's Response to UMF 12, 15; Plaintiff's UMF 23.) Plaintiff claims that when his attorney measured the defect on September 29, 2014, he found the range in elevation to be between 1 inch and 1.5 inches high. (PUMF 24; Ex. 1 Sussman Decl. paragraph 8, 9.) The photographs submitted by Plaintiff also raise a disputed issue as to whether the height of the defect is less than 1 inch. (Plaintiff's Exhibit 1F.) Even if the court were to conclude that the defect were trivial, a disputed issue of material fact as to whether the defect is trivial is raised by the fact that other people have tripped over the sidewalk in the past and have reported it to the Defendant First Bank, Inc. and the fact that it was a foggy day when Plaintiff tripped. (UMF 24, 26, 27.) Therefore, motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is denied. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
